opinion.h4: BASS v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF VIRGINIA. No. 528. Decided January 26, 1959.
opinion.p: Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Hansen, Charles H. Weston, W. Louise Florencourt, Robert W. Ginnane and Carroll T. Prince, Jr. for the United States and the Interstate Commerce Commission, appellees.
Linwood C. Major, Jr. for the Atlantic Greyhound Corporation et al., appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
358 U.S. 333 LANZA v. NEW JERSEY. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW JERSEY. No. 532. Decided January 26, 1959.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
David D. Furman, Attorney General of New Jersey, and Bernard Hellring for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
